steps.title: Set up linear combination and find coefficients
steps.content:
Express $3x + 5y$ as a linear combination of the two equations:
$$3x + 5y = a(x + 4y) + b(2x + y)$$
Expanding the right side:
$$3x + 5y = (a + 2b)x + (4a + b)y$$
Matching coefficients: $a + 2b = 3$ and $4a + b = 5$. From the first equation, $a = 3 - 2b$. Substituting:
$$4(3 - 2b) + b = 5 \Rightarrow 12 - 7b = 5 \Rightarrow b = 1$$
Therefore $a = 1$.

steps.title: Apply the linear combination
steps.content:
Now substitute the coefficients:
$$3x + 5y = 1 \cdot (x + 4y) + 1 \cdot (2x + y) = 19 + 4 = 23$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
